instruction_summary: After the user pointed out the missing Prompt Log requirement, verify the AGENTS chain and add the missing repo-local log for the already-in-progress issue sweep task.
actual_work: Re-read the root AGENTS and the canonical prompt-log policy, confirmed that repo-local tasks must write logs under docs/ai/prompt-logs, and backfilled the missing log for the unprioritized open issue sweep that had already started without one.

commands.notes: Confirmed the root Prompt Log requirement and repo-local path rule.
Confirmed the canonical Prompt Log storage and timing policy.
Checked required fields before writing the retrospective repo-local log.

tests.notes: Read back the backfilled log after writing it.

token_waste_notes: This follow-up existed only because the required Prompt Log was not created at task start.
Prompt Log backfilling required an avoidable policy/schema reread.
wasted_context: The issue sweep work had to be reconstructed after the fact instead of being logged incrementally.

quality_notes: The repo-local issue sweep now has an explicit Prompt Log trail.
The AGENTS-to-policy chain was revalidated instead of assuming the remembered rule.
risks: Backfilled logs are approximate compared with logs started before work begins.
remaining_concerns: Future repo-local tasks in this workspace should create the log stub before any meaningful exploration.

signals.note: The omission was corrected by explicitly checking the AGENTS-to-policy chain and writing the missing log.
This follow-up and the reconstructed issue sweep log would not have been necessary if the stub had been created at task start.
